signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly list IUD insertion as a service on their website?
Is there a reproductive endocrinologist (REI) listed as a provider on staff at this practice?
signals.note: Friction signal — hard caps at Contender; REI presence indicates specialized fertility center, not office-based OBGYN profile

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice list infertility evaluation or workup as a service they offer?
Does this practice operate its own in-house IVF or ART (assisted reproductive technology) program?
signals.note: Friction signal — hard caps at Contender; in-house IVF/ART routes patients to lab-based pathways, not office-based devices

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice list contraception counseling or family planning as a service?
Does this practice list hormone therapy, PCOS management, or hormone-related services?
Does this appear to be an independent private practice (not hospital-owned, not part of a health system)?
Is this practice affiliated with, owned by, or part of a hospital or health system?
signals.note: Friction signal — caps tier at Needs Verification for operator review

signals.signal_label: Cash-pay / self-pay fertility service line visible
signals.prompt_instruction: Does the website show explicit evidence of a cash-pay, self-pay, or out-of-network capability for fertility or related services? Examples: published self-pay or package pricing for IUI, insemination, or fertility treatment; the phrase 'out-of-network'; statements that a service is not covered by insurance; offering superbills or invoices for reimbursement; or visible third-party patient financing such as CareCredit. Answer yes only when such language is explicitly present on the site.
signals.note: Cash-pay readiness — commercial-fit signal for adoption velocity; tunable, not yet a gate.
